focus: Turn accumulated evidence into ranked design options and acceptance criteria
summary: Ranked candidate improvements by leverage, risk, affected surface, and verification cost. Highest leverage is a mandatory candidate/search ledger enforced by stricter post-dispatch validation, followed by reducer/synthesis persistence of null-search evidence, target-selection narrowing, graph vocabulary expansion, convergence candidate-saturation gates, report changes, and only then higher iteration defaults.

label: Mandatory candidate coverage matrix is the highest-leverage design option
acceptanceCriteria: Non-trivial code/spec review iterations emit candidateMatrix/searchLedger rows
Rows include dimension, bugClass, invariant, producer, consumer, negativeTestSearched, evidenceRefs, disposition
Clean dimensions require cited ruled_out/not_applicable/deferred/blocked rows

label: Stricter post-dispatch validation is the enforcement layer for the matrix
acceptanceCriteria: findingsNew must be severity-count object
findingDetails rich fields are validated when findings exist
candidate/search-ledger rows fail validation when missing or uncited

label: Null-search evidence must survive reducer, dashboard, and synthesis
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard renders search debt and ruled-out candidate classes
Review report includes first-class Search Ledger section
PASS/CONDITIONAL exposes searchCoverage posture

label: Focus narrowing and target-selection ledger should precede broad dimension coverage
acceptanceCriteria: Each iteration records targetSelection
Broad scopes require ranked files/flows/invariants before dimensions count as covered
Unavailable graph or semantic search requires explicit fallback ledger

label: Producer/consumer/invariant graph events are valuable but should follow stable ledger schema
acceptanceCriteria: Graph accepts BUG_CLASS, INVARIANT, PRODUCER, CONSUMER, TEST nodes
Stop blockers can cite missing candidate coverage
Graphless runs use text ledger fallback

label: Candidate-saturation convergence gates should not be added without candidate evidence
acceptanceCriteria: STOP requires candidateCoverage/searchLedgerCoverage for non-trivial targets
Blocked-stop events name missing candidate classes
No-finding ratio is convergence-positive only when clean-search rows exist

label: Synthesis/report changes improve operator visibility but cannot be the only fix
acceptanceCriteria: Report includes Search Ledger before final appendix
Planning packet carries candidate coverage summary
Verdict is paired with searchCoverage

label: Higher/default iterations are a supporting change, not the primary depth fix
acceptanceCriteria: Default increases are scoped to high-risk targets or shipped after candidate gates
Tests show added iterations increase candidate coverage, not only empty dimension passes

summary: Implement in two layers: first add candidate/search-ledger schema plus validator enforcement; then add reducer/dashboard/synthesis visibility and candidate-aware convergence. Graph expansion should follow once row semantics are stable.

A successful design makes absence-of-finding claims as auditable as active findings: every clean dimension has cited rows explaining what bug classes, invariants, producers, consumers, and negative tests were searched or explicitly deferred.
The candidate matrix only changes behavior if post-dispatch validation rejects missing or shallow rows.
Candidate-saturation gates need candidate rows before convergence can safely block or allow STOP.
Graph node expansion should follow a stable text/JSON ledger so graphless fallback and semantics are already defined.
Default iteration and threshold differences matter, but later evidence shows budget increases alone do not define deeper search.

direction: Make higher iteration defaults the primary fix
reason: Rejected as primary. Extra iterations can still produce dimension/severity bookkeeping unless the workflow requires candidate-search evidence.

direction: Make graphEvents mandatory as the first and only fix
reason: Rejected as first step. Graph convergence is valuable, but empty graphs currently return CONTINUE and graph vocabulary does not yet model bug class, invariant, producer, consumer, or test nodes.
Rejected. Reports can expose search coverage, but they need reducer-owned candidate/null-search state produced during iterations; wording alone cannot prove deeper review.
